title: smoky pillowy algerian treacle tagine
description: a rich and comforting breakfast dish featuring smoky flavors and a velvety texture.
instructions: heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
remove the lamb from the saucepan and set aside.
add the onion and garlic to the saucepan and cook until the onion is softened, about 5 minutes.
add the blanched onion and garlic to the lamb and set aside.
add the black treacle, apricot jam, smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan and stir to combine.
add the water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 hour.
add the lamb and any accumulated juices to the saucepan and simmer, covered, for an additional 1 hour.
mix the flour and a little water to make a smooth paste, then stir it into the saucepan to thicken the stew.
serve the smoky pillowy algerian treacle tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
